title: Consent flag should be set to avoid manual input
description: Consent flag should be set to avoid manual inputAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalmaintainabilityCode SmellshellVarious package and software management applications require manual input for execution confirmation by default. This confirmation is usually required when installing, updating, or removing programs and packages. General consent can be given to execute the command without manual input.Why is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoSuppose a package manager invocation is part of a script that is executed automatically, and non-interactive mode is not enabled.
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

checks.message: Consent flag should be set to avoid manual input. Package manager commands should include consent flags to enable non-interactive execution. Use '-n' or '--non-interactive' for gdebi, '-y' for apt/apt-get/yum/dnf, '--non-interactive' for zypper, or '--noconfirm' for pacman.
